expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 2 @conduction/nextcloud-vue section: 'All Conduction apps MUST use the shared @conduction/nextcloud-vue library.' CnDataTable and CnDetailViewLayout (analogous to CnDetailPage) are listed. Admin settings MUST use CnSettingsSection. The requirement is explicit and unconditional.

SKILL.md Step 2: 'Pinia stores extend useObjectStore from the library (with appropriate plugins).' Pinia Store Pattern references pinia-store-pattern.md. The tech stack section confirms 'Pinia for state management (NOT Vuex)'.

SKILL.md Step 2 Router Pattern shows `new Router({ mode: 'history', ... })` with base path, routes, and catch-all redirect. Rules: 'History mode (not hash). Base path includes /index.php/apps/{appname}/'. Vue Router v3 is in the tech stack.
SKILL.md Step 2: 'Use <style scoped> — never unscoped styles.' CSS & Styling Rules section: '<style scoped> on all components.' The code example shows a component with `<style scoped>` block.

notes: All four Vue implementation expectations are explicitly specified: @conduction/nextcloud-vue requirement, useObjectStore for Pinia, Vue Router history mode, and scoped styles. These are core Conduction frontend conventions that naive Claude would not know.
